proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (positive direction).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Patients with mcrpc=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with mcrpc=0 (negative direction).
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with visceral_mets=0 (negative direction).

analyses.result_summary: Olaparib-treated mean pfs=3.829 vs untreated 3.735, difference +0.094 months (Welch t-test).
Pembrolizumab-treated mean pfs=3.805 vs untreated 3.741, diff +0.064 (not significant).
ECOG PS strongly inversely correlated with PFS (Pearson r=-0.388).
PSA inversely correlated with PFS (r=-0.153).
Albumin positively correlated with PFS (r=+0.123).
LDH weakly inversely correlated with PFS (r=-0.017).
mCRPC patients mean PFS lower by 0.521 months vs non-mCRPC.
Visceral mets associated with 0.048 months shorter PFS (small but significant).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between brca2_mutation and treatment_olaparib on pfs_months: BRCA2-mutated patients receiving olaparib have substantially longer PFS than expected from main effects alone.
There is a positive interaction between msi_high and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months: MSI-high patients benefit from pembrolizumab more than MSI-low patients.
There is a negative interaction between ar_v7_positive and treatment_enzalutamide on pfs_months: AR-V7+ patients derive less benefit (or harm) from enzalutamide than AR-V7- patients.
There is a negative interaction between ar_v7_positive and treatment_abiraterone on pfs_months: AR-V7+ patients derive less benefit from abiraterone than AR-V7- patients.
There is a positive interaction between psma_high and treatment_lu177_psma on pfs_months: PSMA-high tumors respond better to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy.
Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (unexpected positive correlation in this dataset).

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction: BRCA2+ olaparib effect +1.545 months vs BRCA2- olaparib effect -0.073. Interaction coefficient +1.619.
No detectable interaction: MSI-high pembro effect -0.265 (NS); MSI-low effect +0.075. Interaction coef -0.340 (NS).
No interaction. AR-V7+ enza effect -0.024; AR-V7- enza effect -0.027. Interaction coef +0.002.
No interaction. AR-V7+ abi effect +0.032; AR-V7- abi effect -0.015. Interaction coef +0.048.
No interaction. PSMA-high Lu177 effect -0.011; PSMA-low effect +0.006. Interaction coef -0.017.
Age strongly POSITIVELY correlated with PFS (r=+0.856). Older patients have longer PFS in this cohort, opposite to typical clinical expectation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for age, ECOG, mCRPC, visceral_mets, PSA, albumin, LDH, the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction term remains a strong positive predictor of pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, age_years remains positively associated with pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, none of the targeted-therapy main effects (enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab) is materially associated with pfs_months.
There is no clinically meaningful interaction between mcrpc and any treatment on pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ age + ecog + mcrpc + visceral + psa + albumin + ldh + brca2 + olaparib + brca2*olaparib + ...
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted BRCA2 x olaparib interaction coefficient +1.456 months, p~0; main effects of BRCA2 and olaparib both null after the interaction is included.
Adjusted age coefficient +0.174 months PFS per year of age, p~0. Effect persists after adjusting for ECOG, mCRPC, labs.

Adjusted main-effect coefficients near zero: enzalutamide -0.006 (p=0.24), abiraterone -0.004 (p=0.49), docetaxel -0.006 (p=0.26), lu177_psma -0.009 (p=0.21), pembrolizumab +0.008 (p=0.47). None is significant.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ mcrpc + tx + mcrpc*tx for each treatment
analyses.result_summary: All mcrpc-by-treatment interactions are small. Largest is docetaxel (+0.080, p=0.042 nominal); the rest are NS. No clinically meaningful effect-modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: None of the listed comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, venous_thromboembolism_history, autoimmune_disease, hepatitis_b/c_history, hiv_positive, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis, interstitial_lung_disease_history) is associated with pfs_months.
Among the routine lab values, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l and total_bilirubin_mg_dl are inversely associated with pfs_months (markers of liver/bone disease burden).
Symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are inversely associated with pfs_months.
Prior therapies (prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy) and prior_lines_of_therapy are inversely associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by each comorbidity
analyses.result_summary: All 15 comorbidities tested produce no significant association with PFS (smallest p=0.06 for depression_anxiety). Effect sizes <0.06 months in either direction.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr for each lab vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Alkaline phosphatase r=-0.012 (p=0.007), total bilirubin r=-0.010 (p=0.03). Statistically significant but tiny effect sizes; most other labs (creatinine, sodium, potassium, calcium, platelets, WBC, ANC, ALC, BMI, vitals) are null.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr per symptom grade vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All symptom grades essentially null: fatigue r=+0.002, pain r=-0.001, dyspnea r=-0.001, cough r=+0.001, appetite_loss r=-0.0003. None significant.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests / Pearson on prior therapy variables
analyses.result_summary: All prior-therapy variables null. prior_chemotherapy diff=-0.010 p=0.59; prior_radiation +0.007 p=0.71; prior_surgery -0.030 p=0.12; prior_lines_of_therapy r=-0.006 p=0.21.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (uninsured shorter than private/medicare).
Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than urban residents.
Among the 25 SNP variables, none shows a real association with pfs_months beyond what is expected by chance.

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across race_ethnicity (asian, black, hispanic, other, white): F=0.564, p=0.689. Group means within 0.06 months of each other.
ANOVA across insurance_type (medicaid, medicare, private, uninsured): F=0.344, p=0.794. No disparity detected on the PFS endpoint.
Rural diff +0.015 months (p=0.50). No effect.

analyses.code: Loop stats.pearsonr over 25 snp_* columns vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: 1 of 25 SNPs nominally significant at p<0.05 (rs4986893, p=0.0145), consistent with chance (Bonferroni threshold p<0.002 met by none). All effect sizes |r|<0.011.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the AR-V7 x enzalutamide interaction predicts pfs_months (negative interaction).
Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the AR-V7 x abiraterone interaction predicts pfs_months (negative interaction).
Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the PSMA-high x lu177-PSMA interaction predicts pfs_months (positive interaction).
Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction remains strongly positive.
The age x pfs_months relationship holds within every ECOG stratum (not confounded by performance status).

analyses.code: OLS within mcrpc==1: pfs ~ arv7 + enza + arv7*enza
analyses.result_summary: Within mCRPC subset, AR-V7+ enza effect -0.062 vs AR-V7- enza effect -0.045. Interaction coef -0.017 (p=0.79). No effect-modification by AR-V7.

analyses.code: OLS within mcrpc==1: pfs ~ arv7 + abi + arv7*abi
analyses.result_summary: Within mCRPC, AR-V7+ abi effect +0.049 vs AR-V7- effect -0.005. Interaction +0.054 (p=0.41). NS.

analyses.code: OLS within mcrpc==1: pfs ~ psma + lu177 + psma*lu177
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.011 (p=0.87) within mCRPC. PSMA-high status does NOT modify Lu177-PSMA benefit on PFS in this dataset.

analyses.code: Within mcrpc==1, mean diffs by BRCA2 and olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Within mCRPC, BRCA2+ olaparib effect +1.548 months vs BRCA2- olaparib effect -0.082. Effect concentrated in BRCA2-mutated subgroup, replicated.

analyses.code: Pearson age vs PFS within each ECOG stratum
analyses.result_summary: ECOG=0 r=+0.932; ECOG=1 r=+0.929; ECOG=2 r=+0.910. Age-PFS positive correlation persists in every ECOG stratum, ruling out simple ECOG confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ECOG x treatment_olaparib interaction predicts pfs_months (poor PS dampens benefit).
Albumin x treatment_olaparib interaction predicts pfs_months.
Number of concomitant treatments (n_tx) is associated with pfs_months.

Among non-prostate-canonical gene alterations (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, tp53_mutation, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss), none is independently associated with pfs_months.
Sex_female=1 patients differ from sex_female=0 patients in pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.037 (p=0.36). No statistically detectable effect-modification of olaparib by ECOG.
Interaction coef -0.104 (p=0.08), borderline. Effect is weak and not robust.
Correlation r=-0.0001 (p=0.988). Number of treatments is unrelated to PFS.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for 13 gene alteration columns vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All 13 gene alterations produce non-significant associations (all p>0.10). Largest absolute mean diff -0.184 for ntrk_fusion (n=261, p=0.16). tp53_mutation (most prevalent at 44.8%) diff=+0.003 (p=0.87).
All 50000 patients are sex_female=0 (male). No comparison possible; consistent with prostate cancer cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The BRCA2 x olaparib effect on pfs_months is similar across all three ECOG strata (i.e., does not require performance-status restriction).
The BRCA2 x olaparib effect is larger in older patients (because PFS itself is longer at older ages, the absolute benefit may scale).
BRCA2_mutation has no independent prognostic effect on pfs_months among patients NOT receiving olaparib.
Olaparib has no independent treatment effect on pfs_months among BRCA2-NEGATIVE patients.
Visceral_mets is more strongly associated with shorter pfs_months in non-mCRPC patients than in mCRPC patients.

analyses.code: Subset BRCA2+ within ECOG strata; mean pfs by olaparib status
analyses.result_summary: ECOG=0: +1.692 (p=9e-13); ECOG=1: +1.430 (p=4e-13); ECOG=2: +1.660 (p=7e-7). Effect is large and consistent across strata.

analyses.code: Subset BRCA2+ within age tertiles; mean pfs by olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Low tertile (30-60.6 yrs): +1.261; mid (60.7-69.4): +1.420; high (69.5-90): +1.571. Modest gradient with age, but effect substantial in all strata.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on BRCA2 within olaparib==0 subset
analyses.result_summary: Among non-olaparib patients, BRCA2+ vs BRCA2- mean pfs diff -0.071 months (p=0.026). Effect sign suggests at most a tiny adverse prognostic association of BRCA2 absent treatment, but the magnitude is trivial.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on olaparib within BRCA2==0 subset
analyses.result_summary: Among BRCA2-negative patients, olaparib vs no-olaparib diff -0.073 months (p=0.019). Marginally significant in the wrong direction; magnitude trivial. No meaningful clinical benefit in BRCA2-WT patients.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on visceral_mets within mcrpc strata
analyses.result_summary: mCRPC=0: visceral diff -0.078 (p=0.020). mCRPC=1: visceral diff -0.020 (p=0.51). Mild evidence visceral disease matters more in non-mCRPC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a comprehensive multivariable OLS model of pfs_months including age, ECOG, mCRPC, visceral_mets, PSA, albumin, LDH, BRCA2, treatment_olaparib, BRCA2 x olaparib interaction, AR-V7, MSI-high, PSMA-high, and the other five treatments, the dominant predictors are age (positive), ECOG (negative), mCRPC (negative), albumin (positive), LDH (negative), PSA (negative), and the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction (positive).
After full multivariable adjustment, treatments enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab show no individual main effect on pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, ar_v7_positive itself is associated with slightly shorter pfs_months (residual prognostic effect, not modified by treatment).
After multivariable adjustment, psma_high is associated with slightly longer pfs_months as a prognostic marker (independent of Lu177 use).

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs ~ const + age + ecog + mcrpc + visceral + psa + albumin + ldh + brca2 + olaparib + brca2*olaparib + arv7 + msi + psma + 5 other treatments)
analyses.result_summary: Final R^2=0.931. Coefficients: age +0.174 (p~0), ecog -1.152 (p~0), mcrpc -0.371 (p~0), visceral -0.021 (p=6e-4), psa -0.0027 (p~0), albumin +0.481 (p~0), ldh -0.0005 (p=4e-62), BRCA2*olaparib +1.456 (p~0).
All five non-olaparib treatment main effects |beta|<0.011, all p>0.17. Confirms no marginal benefit on PFS once prognostic factors are adjusted.
Adjusted ar_v7_positive coef -0.0133 months (p=0.026). Tiny but significant residual negative prognostic association.
Adjusted psma_high coef +0.0177 months (p=0.0003). Small positive prognostic association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all hypotheses tested, the only large, replicable, treatment-relevant signal is the BRCA2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction increasing pfs_months by ~1.5 months.

The strongest prognostic signals for pfs_months are age_years (positive), ECOG_PS (negative), mCRPC (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), and LDH_u_l (negative); together these explain >90% of PFS variance.

Several biomarker-treatment pairings biologically expected to show interaction (msi_high x pembrolizumab, ar_v7_positive x enzalutamide/abiraterone, psma_high x lu177-psma) show no detectable effect-modification on pfs_months in this cohort.
Race_ethnicity, insurance_type, rural_residence, education_years, and the 25 SNP variables show no clinically meaningful association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction coefficient and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2 x olaparib adjusted coefficient +1.456 (p~0); subgroup means show +1.55 month PFS gain in BRCA2+ olaparib-treated vs untreated, vs zero effect in BRCA2-. Replicated across mCRPC status, ECOG strata, and age tertiles.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS R^2 and key coefficients
analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.931 with the 6 prognostic factors plus the BRCA2*olaparib term. Age accounts for the largest single share (univariate r=+0.856).
MSI*pembro interaction p=0.15 (effect even nominally negative); AR-V7*enza p=0.96; AR-V7*abi p=0.33; PSMA*lu177 p=0.74. All NS. Conclusion: predicted clinical interactions absent in this dataset.

analyses.code: ANOVA / Welch / Pearson across socioeconomic and SNP variables
analyses.result_summary: race_ethnicity ANOVA p=0.69; insurance_type ANOVA p=0.79; rural p=0.50; education_years r=-0.004 (p=0.40); 25 SNPs collectively yield 1/25 nominally significant (chance level).
